# v1.6.1

## Features

- None.

## Bug Fixes

- Fixed the independent Automation Center window so it resolves and applies the same persisted theme selection as the main MDE app, including follow-system light/dark behavior.
- Aligned Automation Center root theme attributes and panel surfaces with shared MDE theme tokens instead of prototype-hard-coded colors.
- Improved prototype parity for the Automation Center three-panel layout: Task Stack/filter panel, card-based Signal Stack, and richer Flowline detail panel.
- Expanded Flowline with a titled detail head, clear-selection control, source/flow/engine metadata, three phase cards, and bottom actions for start and decision flows.
- Expanded Signal Stack cards with readable task titles, descriptions, source/flow/workspace metadata, badges, diagnostics hierarchy, and explicit Flowline actions.
- Fixed Automation Center E2E readiness so parity assertions wait for projected ready-state UI instead of the loading-state shell.

## Breaking Changes

- None.

## Maintenance

- Bumped workspace release metadata to `1.6.1` for the root package and desktop app.
- Added and strengthened Automation Center unit, integration, and E2E coverage for theme propagation, theme-token usage, Signal Stack structure, Flowline structure, readiness, component ids, and manual alignment.

## Documentation/User Manual

- Updated the zh-CN Automation Center manual for theme behavior and the prototype-aligned three-panel workflow.
- Updated the zh-CN component-name reference for the new Flowline clear-selection control.

## Verification

- `pnpm run lint`
- `pnpm run typecheck`
- `pnpm run build`
- `pnpm run test:unit`
- `pnpm run test:integration`
- `pnpm run test:e2e`
- `pnpm run docs:build`
- `git diff --check`
- Focused Automation Center E2E was rerun after the full E2E pass and the isolated startup flake passed on rerun without code changes.

## Artifacts

- GitHub Release workflow should publish macOS DMG/ZIP artifacts and Windows NSIS updater artifacts for `v1.6.1`.
- Optional macOS signing and notarization secrets are not required for the no-paid-account release path; macOS artifacts may be unsigned if signing secrets are absent.
